perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that at all times until termination or expiration of this Agreement it will hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved in writing by the other party to this Agreement, and will use the Confidential Information for no purpose other than evaluating or pursuing a business relationship with the other party to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it will use reasonable efforts to hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved in writing by the other party to this Agreement, and will use the Confidential Information solely for evaluating a business relationship with the other party to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'at all times' and 'will hold' to 'reasonable efforts' weakens the confidentiality obligation. This creates an ambiguity and an internal contradiction with Section 13, which grants injunctive relief for breaches. The original text clearly states 'at all times' implying strict liability, while the new wording potentially allows for unintentional disclosures to be excused, making it unclear if injunctive relief applies in such cases, thus creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that its breach of the Agreement will cause irreparable damage and hereby agrees that the other party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement, as well as such further relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction. Additionally, in the event of a breach by the Recipient, the disclosing party shall be entitled to recover the costs of enforcing this Agreement including, without limitation reasonable attorneys' fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates strict confidentiality 'at all times,' implying a strong, unwavering obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'reasonable efforts.' This creates a direct conflict with the 'Contradicted Text,' which grants the other party the right to seek injunctive relief for any breach of the agreement. The 'reasonable efforts' standard potentially allows for unintentional disclosures, making it unclear whether injunctive relief would apply in such instances. This introduces ambiguity and makes the 'Contradicted Text' regarding injunctive relief less certain, and thus creates an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement with respect to any portion of the other party's Confidential Information shall terminate when the Recipient can document that: (a) it was in the public domain at the time it was <PAGE> 2 communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (b) it entered the public domain subsequent to the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party through no fault of the Recipient; (c) it was in the Recipient's possession free of any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (d) it was rightfully communicated to the Recipient free of any obligation of confidence subsequent to the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party or (e) it was communicated by the other party to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement with respect to any portion of the other party's Confidential Information shall terminate when the Recipient believes in good faith that: (a) it was generally known at the time it was <PAGE> 2 communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (b) it became generally known subsequent to the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (c) it was in the Recipient's possession without any restriction at the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (d) it was rightfully received without any restriction subsequent to the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party or (e) it was communicated by the other party to an affiliated third party without any restriction.
perturbation.explanation: The original termination conditions required documentation. The modification replaces objective 'can document' with subjective 'believes in good faith,' and replaces 'public domain' with 'generally known' which is vague, as well as changing 'unaffiliated third party' to 'affiliated third party.' This introduces a contradiction with Section 2, which states the information must be held in 'strict confidence.' The subjective standard now makes it difficult to enforce confidentiality, as the Recipient's 'good faith' belief is hard to challenge even if information isn't truly public.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that at all times until termination or expiration of this Agreement it will hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved in writing by the other party to this Agreement, and will use the Confidential Information for no purpose other than evaluating or pursuing a business relationship with the other party to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the above, the party to whom Confidential Information was disclosed (the "RECIPIENT") shall not be in violation of this Section 3 with regard to a disclosure that was in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, provided that the Recipient provides the other party with prior written notice of such disclosure in order to permit the other party to seek confidential treatment of such information. Each party shall only permit access to Confidential Information of the other party to those of its employees or authorized representatives having a need to know and who have signed confidentiality agreements or are otherwise bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the objective 'can document' with the subjective 'believes in good faith,' and replaces 'public domain' with 'generally known,' and 'unaffiliated third party' to 'affiliated third party.' This directly contradicts the requirement in the 'Contradicted Text' that each party holds Confidential Information in 'strict confidence.' The subjective standard introduced by the changed text, specifically 'believes in good faith,' makes it significantly harder to enforce the confidentiality obligations as it is difficult to challenge a party's assertion of good faith belief, even if the information isn't truly public or was disclosed to an affiliated third party, creating a clear and strong contradiction. The original text also requires documentation, which is objective, while the changed text only requires the Recipient to believe in good faith, which is subjective. This makes it difficult to enforce the 'strict confidence' requirement from the contradicted text, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, or upon written request of the other party, each party shall promptly destroy or return to the other all documents and other tangible materials representing the other's Confidential Information and all copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, each party shall make a reasonable effort to destroy or return to the other all documents and other tangible materials representing the other's Confidential Information and all copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces 'shall promptly destroy or return' with 'shall make a reasonable effort to destroy or return.' This introduces ambiguity. The original text creates a clear obligation to destroy or return. The changed wording allows for interpretation and potentially excuses incomplete destruction or return due to 'reasonable' limitations. This change directly contradicts the original intent of Sections 1 and 2 to protect CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, because under the modified obligation, failure to return materials representing CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be considered as a reasonabe and thus, not be consider a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information provided by either party to the other, including but not limited to (a) patent and patent applications, (b) trade secret, and (c) proprietary information, ideas, techniques, sketches, drawings, works of authorship, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatuses, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future, and proposed products and services of each of the parties, and including, without limitation, their respective information concerning research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, investors, employees, business and contractual relationships, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, marketing plans and information the disclosing party provides regarding third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a clear action ('shall promptly destroy or return'), ensuring the protection of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION as defined in the contradicted text. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'make a reasonable effort,' creating ambiguity and a potential loophole. This directly contradicts the intent to safeguard CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The change allows for interpretations where failure to return materials might be excused, conflicting with the expectation of strict protection outlined in the contradicted section, thus creating an in-text contradiction as defined in the provided explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Although the disclosing party has endeavored to include in the Confidential Information, information known to it which it believes to be relevant for the purpose of the Recipient's investigation of a potential transaction, the Recipient acknowledges and agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its employees or representatives have made or make any representations or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of all or any portion of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its employees or representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient or any of the Recipient's employees or representatives resulting from the use of, or conclusions arising from, the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosing party makes no representation that the Confidential Information is complete, accurate, or relevant for the Recipient's investigation of a potential transaction. The Recipient agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its employees or representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient or any of the Recipient's employees or representatives resulting from the use of, or conclusions arising from, the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause acknowledged an effort to provide relevant information. The modified clause states there is no representation of accuracy, completeness or relevance, essentially disclaiming responsibility for the quality of the Confidential Information provided. This directly contradicts the purpose and intent of Section 2, which states that the confidentiality is held while the party is using the information to evaluate the pursuing of a business relationship, as it implies the disclosing party can provide unreliable or misleading information, undermining the value and intent of the NDA, and the clause to evaluate for the pursuit of a business relationship with an unreliable foundation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that at all times until termination or expiration of this Agreement it will hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved in writing by the other party to this Agreement, and will use the Confidential Information for no purpose other than evaluating or pursuing a business relationship with the other party to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the above, the party to whom Confidential Information was disclosed (the "RECIPIENT") shall not be in violation of this Section 3 with regard to a disclosure that was in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, provided that the Recipient provides the other party with prior written notice of such disclosure in order to permit the other party to seek confidential treatment of such information. Each party shall only permit access to Confidential Information of the other party to those of its employees or authorized representatives having a need to know and who have signed confidentiality agreements or are otherwise bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the acknowledgement of effort to provide relevant information and states there is no representation of accuracy, completeness or relevance of the Confidential Information. The contradicted text states that the Confidential Information will be used for evaluating or pursuing a business relationship with the other party. The change now implies that the disclosing party can provide unreliable or misleading information, undermining the value and intent of the NDA, and the clause to evaluate for the pursuit of a business relationship. Therefore, the change creates a direct contradiction, as the purpose of evaluating/pursuing a business relationship becomes significantly compromised if the information provided is not represented to be accurate, complete, or relevant.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties agrees that the software programs of the other party contain valuable confidential information and each party agrees it will not modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the Confidential Information of the other party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties agrees that the software programs of the other party may contain confidential information, and each party agrees to make reasonable efforts not to modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the Confidential Information of the other party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The changes weakens the protection of software. Replacing 'contain valuable confidential information' with 'may contain confidential information' and 'it will not' with 'to make reasonable efforts not to' reduces the perceived value of the software and the obligation to protect it. This undermines the strict prohibitions in Section 8, which prohibits the use of Confidential Information to 'make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from any Confidential Information', since even if the software is reverse enginnered it was only a 'reasonable' effort and 'may not' be valuable
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties recognize and agree that nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any property rights, by license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information of the other party disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, or to any invention or any patent, copyright, trademark, or other intellectual property right that has issued or that may issue, based on such Confidential Information. Neither party shall make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from any Confidential Information to the other party. 2
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the protection of software. Replacing 'contain valuable confidential information' with 'may contain confidential information' and 'it will not' with 'to make reasonable efforts not to' reduces the perceived value of the software and the obligation to protect it. This undermines the strict prohibitions in the contradicted text. Specifically, the contradicted text states 'Neither party shall make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from any Confidential Information to the other party', which is a strict prohibition. The changed text, however, only requires 'reasonable efforts not to modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble', thus introducing a contradiction as it allows for a scenario where the Confidential Information may be used in a way that the contradicted text prohibits, especially if only 'reasonable efforts' were made to prevent it.
